title: Pandanus butyrophorus
intro: Pandanus butyrophorus, commonly known as the screwpine or Pandanus, is a species of flowering plant in the family Pandanaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures to treat various health issues, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pandanus butyrophorus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Pandanus butyrophorus is often used to prepare herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat conditions like fever, respiratory infections, and gastrointestinal issues.
taxonomy: Pandanus butyrophorus is a species of plant in the family Pandanaceae. It is a type of pandan, a genus of about 130 species of tropical plants. These plants are native to the Indian Ocean and Pacific regions, particularly in India and Southeast Asia. The Pandanaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as screw pines or pandans. Pandanus butyrophorus is often used as an ornamental plant due to its unique foliage and growth habit.
